blockers.description: Pre-existing CI failures block all PRs: Prettier formatting in packages/storage/src/{aggregation-queries.ts,migrations.ts,sqlite-store.ts} and apps/cli/src/commands/inspect.ts

blockers.requiredAction: Run `pnpm format:fix` on affected files and push to fix CI across all open PRs

escalations: HIGH: CI broken across all open PRs — pre-existing Prettier formatting failures block entire merge queue. Engineering must fix `pnpm format:fix` before anything can land.
HIGH: Content agents silent for 4 consecutive cycles (#995). EM is now the sole content executor. Requires ops/HQ intervention for permanent fix.
MEDIUM: OWASP blog (#925) drafted directly by EM after 3 cycles of escalation. Requires founder review + LinkedIn publish to close.
MEDIUM: Weekly content pack #1074 (v2.8.0 / Go kernel) has 5 LinkedIn posts and 2 blog outlines — high-value material going to waste without a publishing agent.
LOW: #1084 (v3.0 user capture funnel) is a v3.0 gate. Zero capture mechanisms on site. This must ship before v3.0 announce.
productInsights: v2.8.0 (4 CLI adapters: Claude, Codex, Copilot, Gemini) is the strongest marketing story this month — 'one policy file governs all major AI coding agents'
Go kernel fast-path (~100x faster) + v2.8.0 multi-driver governance = a compelling dual story for platform engineers
The CI breakage from Prettier is a dogfood story: our own governance didn't catch unformatted code — highlighting why pre-commit hooks matter
OWASP blog angle: honest 64/100 vs competitors claiming self-reported 10/10 across the board — authenticity is the differentiator
OpenCode adapter (#1109) has do-not-merge — important not to publicize until it lands cleanly
